message:
feat(experiment): expand A/B testing system with full lifecycle management

Major expansion of the skill experiment system to support complete experiment
lifecycle management, including assignment tracking, event recording, and
statistical analysis for concluding experiments.

New subcommands in src/cli/commands/experiment.rs:
- `experiment status <id>`: Display experiment state, variant metrics, and
  current allocation probabilities from Thompson Sampling bandit
- `experiment assign <id>`: Get deterministic variant assignment for current
  session using consistent hashing (session_id + experiment_id)
- `experiment load <id>`: Load the skill variant assigned to current session,
  integrating with progressive disclosure system
- `experiment record <id> --event <type> --variant <id>`: Record engagement
  events (view, use, success, failure, feedback) with optional metrics JSON
- `experiment conclude <id>`: Analyze results and recommend winner based on
  statistical significance, generates conclusion report

New migration (008_add_skill_experiment_events.sql):
- skill_experiment_events table for event tracking:
  - id (TEXT PRIMARY KEY)
  - experiment_id, variant_id (foreign keys)
  - event_type (view|use|success|failure|feedback)
  - metrics_json (optional structured metrics)
  - context_json (optional context data)
  - session_id (for user journey tracking)
  - created_at timestamp

Storage layer additions (src/storage/sqlite.rs):
- ExperimentEventRecord struct for event persistence
- insert_experiment_event(): Record new events
- get_experiment_events(): Query events by experiment/variant/type
- get_experiment_event_counts(): Aggregate counts for analysis
- get_experiment_metrics_summary(): Statistical summaries per variant

Thompson Sampling integration:
- Bandit-based allocation adjusts variant probabilities based on outcomes
- Posterior updates from success/failure events
- Exploration-exploitation balance for optimal learning

Use cases:
- Test alternative skill formulations for effectiveness
- Compare different progressive disclosure strategies
- A/B test skill organization and tagging approaches
- Measure impact of content changes on user success rates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+-- Add experiment event tracking for A/B testing
+CREATE TABLE skill_experiment_events (
+    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
+    experiment_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES skill_experiments(id),
+    variant_id TEXT NOT NULL,
+    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,        -- assign | outcome | conclude
+    metrics_json TEXT,               -- JSON object
+    context_json TEXT,               -- JSON object
+    session_id TEXT,
+    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
+);
+
+CREATE INDEX idx_experiment_events_experiment ON skill_experiment_events(experiment_id);
+CREATE INDEX idx_experiment_events_variant ON skill_experiment_events(experiment_id, variant_id);
